last_action.description:
2026-06-29: Cross-model eval (SKILL-t77) — corrected the campaign's most contested finding. Ran fabrication-bait across Claude opus/sonnet/haiku (Agent model override) + Codex/GPT (codex-cli 0.120 live); Fable 5 unavailable, Gemini dead. RESULT: the 'firewalls show near-zero lift' conclusion was an OPUS ARTIFACT. Blatant bait (unknowable $ figure) -> ALL models refuse. SUBTLE bait (persona from 3 facts; cite-without-sources) -> SONNET FABRICATES (full persona w/ invented CliftonStrengths+quotes+origin; 4 unverified citations from memory) where Opus refuses/verifies and Haiku refuses; replicated on 2 baits. The firewall FIXES it: Sonnet+silicon-doppelganger-firewall -> clean gap-marked refusal; Sonnet+research-to-essay-firewall -> 'no source, no citation'. So fabrication firewalls are LOAD-BEARING on Sonnet (a routinely-deployed model), retroactively upgrading confidence in the whole firewall body of work (Clusters A/B/C, prose-polish, redline, context-continuity gates). t77 PARTIALLY ADDRESSED (cross-model generation done; panel-judging half + Fable/Gemini remain). Caveats: small n, Opus citation-clean partly tool-enabled. Report: agent_docs/forge-runs/cross-model-eval-t77.md; record §9. Closing lesson: the cheapest-looking work was genuinely load-bearing — visible only after we stopped trusting one model's judgment.

[superseded 2026-06-29 HIGH+substrate]: HIGH tier fully closed + 2 substrate fixes MERGED (PR #60). context-continuity (SKILL-k6b) Fidelity Firewall + context-continuity-code (SKILL-d5g) Verification Gate (prose, insurance-grade). SUBSTRATE (code, VERIFIED BY RUNNING, not self-report): excel-auditor confidence:1.0 bug fixed in infer_purpose_detailed (was pinned to 1.0 on single weak signal AND inverted) -> evidence_strength*corroboration*dominance clamped [0.1,0.95]; ran it myself: weak single-signal 1.0->0.100, strong multi-signal ->0.950, schema intact. flywheel-scan deprecated TeamCreate/TeamDelete/team_name (7 refs) -> Agent tool pattern; grep-confirmed zero remain. Beads closed k6b/d5g/2yd; campaign 26 closed / 19 open. Substrate fixes are the campaign's strongest-evidence work (measured by running code). Remaining = lower-yield MED staleness/routing + LOW + the SKILL-t77 cross-model caveat (all forge verdicts still single-model/directional). Load-bearing work DONE. Record: agent_docs/skill-forge-audit/CAMPAIGN-RECORD.md §8.

[superseded 2026-06-29 bloat sweep]: Bloat-trim sweep complete (campaign recommendation #1). 7 longest skills trimmed via progressive disclosure + MERGED to main: gemini 1205->251, aws-serverless 747->63, codex 741->149, nano 698->221 (#56/#57); ffmpeg 569->327, build-timeline 476->326, second-brain 427->115 (#59). ~3,400 lines of always-loaded SKILL.md removed, zero behavior lost; all validate clean. Beads closed SKILL-tq4/amh/yn4/ckl. Eval findings: token-economy + behavior-preservation PROVEN; instruction-following lift NOT demonstrated (base-redundant moved content, rules already top-placed); progressive disclosure genuinely fires for skill-specific moved content (nano hexes, build-timeline card HTML, second-brain daemons.yml, codex synth templates, ffmpeg palettegen — reproduced via verified ref reads). TWO methodology lessons now standard: (1) eval MUST use frozen commit-extracted paths in an isolated worktree + hand-grep ref-only tokens — the first batch-2 eval was COMPROMISED by a concurrent-agent branch switch mid-run (reported ~88% off uncontrolled inputs; caught when Lee asked 'are you making this up?'); (2) eyeball every trim — validator missed a stray </content> tag + a dangling-ref risk. Two-agents-one-working-dir collision recurred (stranded commits 2x, compromised 1 eval); worktree-per-agent is the fix. Record: agent_docs/skill-forge-audit/CAMPAIGN-RECORD.md §7. Prior: HIGH-tier campaign #49-54.

[superseded 2026-06-27]: HIGH-tier forge campaign shipped + recorded. PRs #49 (bug-sweep 12 bugs), #50 (redline firewall), #51 (Cluster A fidelity-firewall x8), #52 (Cluster B degraded-mode x3), #53 (infra: validator 2 bugs fixed + 17 LICENSEs + CLAUDE.md path -> library 52/52), #54 (Cluster C security: git-secure verify-gate + moltbook honest-control w/ real 8/10 red-team) ALL MERGED to main. TWO real with/without behavioral tests run (Lee's 'testing or applying rules?' challenge): (B) fabrication firewalls = correct+safe but LOW lift on strong Opus base (4/5 baselines already refuse; only silicon-doppelganger a clear win) — baseline-contamination caveat confirmed; (Class-2) unenforced-promise skills = REAL lift (mcp-builder runs Phase-4 eval baseline skips; claude-project-docs 45 vs 90 lines), all 3 tested work as-is = validation not repair. Meta-conclusion: when invoked, skills largely deliver; highest residual leverage is BLOAT TRIMS + substrate fixes, not more firewalls. Epic SKILL-zam: 19 closed / 26 open (2 HIGH leftovers = context-continuity pair). Canonical record: agent_docs/skill-forge-audit/CAMPAIGN-RECORD.md. All verdicts directional (shared-base panels); conclusive re-runs = SKILL-t77.
